body: #USAGE arg "<file>"                             // positional arg, completed as a filename
#USAGE arg "<dir>"                              // positional arg, completed as a directory
#USAGE arg "<file>" default="file.txt"          // default value for arg
#USAGE arg "<file>" env="MY_FILE"               // arg can be backed by an env var
#USAGE arg "<file>" parse="mycli parse-file {}" // parse arg value with external command
#USAGE arg "[file]" var=#true // multiple args can be passed (e.g. mycli file1 file2 file3) (0 or more)
#USAGE arg "<file>" var=#true // multiple args can be passed (e.g. mycli file1 file2 file3) (1 or more)
#USAGE arg "<file>" var=#true var_min=3 // at least 3 args must be passed
#USAGE arg "<file>" var=#true var_max=3 // up to 3 args can be passed
#USAGE   choices "bash" "zsh" "fish" // <shell> must be one of the choices
#USAGE   choices env="DEPLOY_ENVS" // values from \$DEPLOY_ENVS, split on commas and/or whitespace
#USAGE arg "<file>" long_help="longer help for --help (as oppoosed to -h)"
#USAGE arg "<file>" double_dash="required" // arg must be passed after a double dash (e.g. mycli -- file.txt)
#USAGE arg "<file>" double_dash="optional" // arg may be passed after a double dash (e.g. mycli -- file.txt or mycli file.txt)
#USAGE arg "<file>..." double_dash="automatic" // once arg is passed, behave as if a double dash was passed (e.g. mycli file.txt --filewithdash)
#USAGE arg "<args>..." double_dash="preserve" // preserve double dashes as args (e.g. mycli arg1 -- arg2 -- arg3)
#USAGE   alias "conf" hide=#true // hide alias from docs and completions
#USAGE cmd "config" hide=#true // hide command from docs and completions
#USAGE cmd "config" subcommand_required=#true // subcommand is not optional
#// all help fields can be either inline params or separate nodes like
#// below for the *_long_help fields. Typically when a lot of space is needed
#// it's cleaner to use separate nodes.
#// use a custom completion command for all args named "plugin"
#// descriptions split from completions on ":"; escape with "\"
#USAGE flag "-u --user <user>" // one way to define a flag
#USAGE flag "--user" { // another way to define the same flag
#USAGE flag "--user" { alias "-u" hide=#true } // hide alias from docs and completions
#USAGE flag "-f --force" global=#true          // global can be set on any subcommand
#USAGE flag "--file <file>" default="file.txt" // default value for flag
#USAGE flag "-v --verbose" count=#true         // instead of true/false \$usage_verbose is # of times
#USAGE flag "--include <pattern>" var=#true            // flag can be repeated (--include a --include b)
#USAGE flag "--include... <pattern>"                   // same as above, ellipsis on flag
#USAGE flag "--include <pattern>..."                   // arg is variadic (--include a b c in one invocation)
#USAGE flag "--include <pattern>" var=#true var_min=1  // at least 1 value required
#USAGE flag "--include <pattern>" var=#true var_max=5  // up to 5 values allowed
#USAGE flag "--color" env="MYCLI_COLOR" // flag can be backed by an env var
#USAGE flag "--color" config="ui.color" // flag can be backed by a config file
#USAGE flag "--file <file>"  // args named "<file>" will be completed as files
#USAGE flag "--dir <dir>"    // args named "<dir>" will be completed as directories
#USAGE flag "--file <file>" required_if="--dir"     // if --dir is set, --file must also be set
#USAGE flag "--file <file>" required_unless="--dir" // either --file or --dir must be present
#USAGE flag "--file <file>" overrides="--stdin"     // if --file is set, previous --stdin will be ignored
#USAGE   choices "bash" "zsh" "fish" // <shell> must be one of the choices
#USAGE   choices env="DEPLOY_ENVS" // values from \$DEPLOY_ENVS, split on commas and/or whitespace
#USAGE flag "--file <file>" long_help="longer help for --help (as opposed to -h)"
#// this is equivalent to the above but preferred when a lot of space is needed
#USAGE   long_help r#"longer help for --help (as opposed to -h)
#USAGE min_usage_version "1.0.0" // the minimum version of usage this CLI supports
#USAGE                           // you want this at the top
#USAGE name "My CLI"        // a friendly name for the CLI
#USAGE bin "mycli"          // the name of the binary
#USAGE version "1.0.0"      // the version of the CLI
#USAGE author "nobody"      // the author of the CLI
#USAGE license "MIT"        // SPDX license the CLI is released under
#// examples (shown in markdown and manpage docs)
#USAGE example "mycli --help" header="Getting help" help="Display help information"
#// render a link to the source code in markdown docs
#USAGE include file="./my_overrides.usage.kdl" // include another spec, will be merged and override existing values
